title: Create a HashMap
instruction:
Import `HashMap` from `std::collections`. Use `insert` to add entries.

Run this.
successMessage: get returns Option<&V> — Some if found, None if not. HashMap keys must implement Eq and Hash.

title: Iterate a HashMap
instruction:
Iterating gives (key, value) pairs. Order is not guaranteed.

Run this (output order may vary).
successMessage: We sorted the pairs by key so output is deterministic. HashMap iteration order is random — always sort if you need predictable output.

title: contains_key and remove
instruction:
Check existence with `contains_key`. Remove with `remove`.

Run this.
successMessage: remove returns Option<V> with the removed value. Here we discard it. contains_key is O(1) just like get.

title: entry API
instruction:
The `entry` API updates a value or inserts a default elegantly:

```rust
map.entry(key).or_insert(default)
```

Run this.
successMessage: entry().or_insert(0) either gets the existing value or inserts 0. Then we dereference and increment. Classic word-count pattern.

title: HashMap from iterator
instruction:
Build a HashMap from an iterator of (key, value) pairs using `.collect()`.

Run this.
successMessage: zip combines two iterators into pairs. collect turns them into a HashMap. map["b"] is indexing — it panics if key is missing, unlike get.

title: Counting with HashMap
instruction:
A common pattern: count frequencies.

Run this.
successMessage: into_iter() consumes the HashMap, giving owned (K, V) pairs. The sort works on tuples (sorts by first element, then second).

title: Nested HashMap
instruction:
HashMap values can be any type, including other HashMaps.

Run this.

codeChecks.message: Use or_default() to initialise the inner HashMap when first inserting.

successMessage: or_default() inserts and returns a reference to a default value (empty HashMap here). Output order varies — HashMap is unordered.

title: HashMap vs Vec — when to use which
instruction:
Use Vec when order matters or data is indexed by position.
Use HashMap when you look up by a meaningful key.

Run this.
successMessage: Vec for sequences, HashMap for lookups. Choosing the right data structure is one of the most impactful decisions in a program.
